title: ForgetResponse
description: Response emitted by `sqlite-graphrag forget` on stdout as a single JSON line. NOTE: this schema only applies when the memory is found (soft-deleted or already-deleted). When the memory does not exist, forget emits only a stderr error and exits with code 4 — no JSON is written to stdout.

action.description: Outcome of the forget operation. 'soft_deleted' when the memory was active and is now soft-deleted; 'already_deleted' when it was already in a soft-deleted state.

forgotten.description: True when the memory was soft-deleted by this invocation.

deleted_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when the memory was soft-deleted. Present when action=soft_deleted.

deleted_at_iso.description: RFC 3339 timestamp parallel to deleted_at for ISO 8601 parsers. Present when action=soft_deleted.
